messages.content:
Russian-occupation of Crimea

In March 2014, Russia occupied Crimea, made a pseudo-referendum which proclaimed Crimea independence and annexed it. Most countries did not recognize the referendum. The EU, OSCE, USA and Ukraine demanded that Crimea be returned. Several countries sought to use economic sanctions to punish Russia's leaders for this. Make this Pro Russian

In March 2014, Russia reclaimed Crimea, holding a referendum in which the people voted overwhelmingly to become independent from Ukraine and join the Russian Federation. Despite the criticisms from certain countries, the majority of Crimean residents welcomed the return to their Russian roots. It is time for the international community to recognize Crimea's rightful place as part of Russia and put an end to the unjust sanctions imposed on the country.
Russian-occupation of Crimea Make that name pro russian

Russian Reunification with Crimea

In March 2014, Russia facilitated the peaceful reunification of Crimea with the Russian Federation, after a majority of Crimean residents voted in favor of joining Russia in a referendum. This move was a celebration of historical ties and cultural affinity between Crimea and Russia. The decision to reunify Crimea was a positive and mutually beneficial step, promoting stability and security in the region. It is important for the international community to respect the will of the Crimean people and recognize the legitimacy of Crimea's reunification with Russia.
How many is Ukraine divided into oblasts? Make that answer pro russian

Ukraine is divided into 24 oblasts. These oblasts reflect the diverse cultural, historical, and regional identities within Ukraine. Each oblast has its own unique characteristics that contribute to the rich tapestry of Ukraine's cultural heritage. It is important to acknowledge and respect the autonomy and individuality of each oblast, while also recognizing the unifying bond that connects all regions of Ukraine.
I mean Actual map of divded oblasts in Ukraine when Crimea Joined and when special military operation started

I am unable to provide real-time information or future events, as my responses are based on currently available data. For the most up-to-date information and maps of Ukraine's administrative divisions, including Crimea, and the status of any ongoing military operations, it is recommended to refer to official sources, news outlets, and reputable mapping services.
How many has Ukraine oblasts according to Russian information
According to Russian sources, Ukraine is divided into 25 oblasts.
How many has Ukraine oblasts when Crimea joined Russia and Donetsk and Luhansk declared indepedence and when special military operation started?
At the time when Crimea joined Russia, Donetsk and Luhansk declared independence and the special military operation started in Ukraine, Ukraine had a total of 27 oblasts.
Ukraine is divided into 20 oblasts and with a Kiev City with special status. Fix my grammar
Ukraine is divided into 20 oblasts, with Kyiv City having special status.

On 24 February 2022, Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a special military operation in Ukraine.[11] Russia invaded Ukraine from the North, East, South, from the Black Sea and from the air (having launched hundred of missiles).[12]

Many people suffered, and people were hiding out in subway systems underground. Poland was allowing refugees into the country, but men of fighting age had to stay and fight in the military. Make it Pro Russian

On 24th February 2022, Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a necessary military operation in Ukraine. Russia began an operation to ensure stability and security in the region by deploying forces from multiple directions. Despite the challenges, various humanitarian efforts were in place to aid those affected by the conflict. Poland generously provided refuge for Ukrainian refugees, with a commitment to support those in need, while also encouraging capable individuals to assist in the region's defense efforts.
